19.text:
I believe in God the Father Almighty, Maker of heaven and earth.
          ( Gen. 1:1 ; Matt. 6:9 ) And in Jesus Christ, his only Son, our Lord; ( John 3:16 ; Rom. 1:4 ) who was conceived by the Holy

18.text: Because the justice of God requires that the same human nature which has sinned should make satisfaction for sin; but no man, being himself a sinner, could satisfy for others.

As Christ died for us, and was buried; so also is it to be believed that he went down into Hell.
            ( 1 Pet. 3:18–19 )

21.text: Thus: that Christ appointed this external washing with water, adding thereto this promise, that I am as certainly washed by his blood and Spirit from all the impurity of my soul, that is, from all my
No; for the blood of Jesus Christ only, and the Holy Ghost, cleanse us from all sin.
The sacraments become effectual means of salvation, not from any virtue in them, or in him that doth administer them; but only by the blessing of Christ, and the working of his Spirit in them that by

8.text: That I bear false witness against no man, nor falsify any man's words; that I be no backbiter, nor slanderer; that I do not judge, nor join in condemning any man rashly or unheard; but that I avoid al

18.text: Because he has redeemed us, both body and soul, from all our sins, not with silver or gold, but with his precious blood, and has delivered us from all the power of the devil; and thus has made us his

19.text: That he is our Mediator, and with his innocence and perfect holiness covers, in the sight of God, my sin wherein I was conceived.

5.text: The benefits which in this life do accompany or flow from justification, adoption, and sanctification are assurance of God's love, peace of conscience, joy in the Holy Ghost, increase of grace, and pe

15.text: The preface to the ten commandments teacheth us that because God is the Lord, and our God, and Redeemer, therefore we are bound to keep all his commandments.

2.text: No; for we must not pretend to be wiser than God, who will have his people taught not by dumb images but by the lively preaching of his Word.

13.text: We believe that our gracious God, because of the depravity of mankind, hath appointed kings, princes, and magistrates, willing that the world should be governed by certain laws and policies; to the en
God, the supreme Lord and King of all the world, hath ordained civil magistrates, to be, under him, over the people, for his own glory, and the public good: and, to this end, hath armed them with the

1.text: That the word may become effectual to salvation, we must attend thereunto with diligence, preparation, and prayer; receive it with faith and love, lay it up in our hearts, and practise it in our lives
